title: Job.java
description:
Job.java is a central class in Hadoop MapReduce that encapsulates the entire job configuration and execution. It extends the Configurable class and implements the JobContext interface. The Job class manages job-specific configurations, sets input and output paths, specifies the job name, and defines the job's classes (like Mapper and Reducer classes, their key and value classes). It also handles job setup, cleanup, and the job's lifecycle. The Job class interacts with the JobTracker (now deprecated in favor of YARN) to submit, track, and manage the job's execution.

TITLE: Task.java

DESCRIPTION: Task is an abstract class in Hadoop MapReduce that represents a single unit of work. It extends the Configurable class and implements the TaskContext interface. Task is responsible for managing the task's lifecycle, including initialization, execution, and cleanup. It provides methods to set up and tear down the task environment, handle input and output operations, and manage task attempts. The Task class interacts with the TaskTracker (now deprecated in favor of YARN) to submit, track, and manage the task's execution.

TITLE: TaskAttemptContextImpl.java

DESCRIPTION: TaskAttemptContextImpl.java provides the context for a single attempt of a task in Hadoop MapReduce. It extends the TaskContext and implements the TaskAttemptContext interface. This class manages the task attempt's configuration, provides access to input and output operations, and handles task-specific counters and progress. It interacts with Hadoop's configuration, task attempt ID, and provides methods to retrieve and set input keys and values, and write output keys and values.

TITLE: TaskReport.java

DESCRIPTION: TaskReport.java, as previously described, encapsulates the state of a task in Apache Hadoop MapReduce, including its ID, progress, state, diagnostics, start and finish times, counters, and status.

TITLE: TaskUmbilical.java

DESCRIPTION: TaskUmbilical.java is a part of the legacy MapReduce implementation, responsible for providing a communication channel between the Map and Reduce tasks and the JobTracker. It allows tasks to report progress, counters, and status to the JobTracker.

TITLE: OutputCommitter.java

DESCRIPTION: OutputCommitter.java is responsible for coordinating the commit process of a task in Hadoop MapReduce. It ensures that the task's output is written correctly and that any necessary cleanup operations are performed. It interacts with the FileSystem and TaskAttemptContext to manage the output directory.

TITLE: FileOutputFormat.java

DESCRIPTION: FileOutputFormat.java, as previously described, manages output directories, handles compression, and generates unique file names in Hadoop MapReduce.

TITLE: MultipleOutputs.java

DESCRIPTION: MultipleOutputs.java, as previously described, simplifies writing output data to multiple outputs in a MapReduce job.

--- DIRECTORY: io ---

TITLE: Mapper.java

DESCRIPTION: Mapper.java is the core class in Hadoop MapReduce for processing input data. It extends the Configured class and implements the Mapper interface. The Mapper class is responsible for processing input key-value pairs, emitting processed key-value pairs, and maintaining the progress within the task. It interacts with the MapContext (now deprecated) for input and output operations.

TITLE: Reducer.java

DESCRIPTION: Reducer.java is the core class in Hadoop MapReduce for processing and aggregating data. It extends the Configured class and implements the Reducer interface. The Reducer class is responsible for processing grouped key-value pairs from the
